summary: Modules 5 (Booking Management) & 6 (Vehicle Allotment) backend testing complete. Created /app/backend/tests/test_modules_5_6.py with 18 tests covering every requirement in the review request. Initial run surfaced a classic Motor ObjectId-leak bug on POST /leads/{lid}/booking, POST /bookings/{bid}/payments and POST /bookings/{bid}/allotment (insert_one mutates the dict in-place adding _id then the handler returned that dict, causing HTTP 500 with 'ObjectId is not iterable'). Fixed in-place via `doc.pop('_id', None)` on those 3 endpoints (explicitly allowed by the fix-scope rules). After fix: 18/18 new tests PASS, and the Module 3/4 regression suite (14/14) still passes.

minor.issue: When sales_executive sends protected fields (booking_date/status), they are silently stripped — test passes, but consider returning a 200 with a warning header or explicit field echo so the UI doesn't show stale data post-submit.

doc['status'] is hard-coded to 'Allotted' at creation even though ALLOTMENT_STATUSES contains 'Pending' — spec implies Pending could be an initial state. Currently acceptable but worth confirming with product.

action_items: Fix applied by testing agent: pop _id after insert_one on 3 booking/payment/allotment endpoints (server.py lines 1345, 1491, 1552). Review and keep or switch to explicit projection.
Consider a global response encoder / helper that strips _id automatically to prevent future regressions (pattern already caused 500s here).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 1894 lines — continuing the iteration-2 recommendation: split into routers (auth, leads, followups, deals, bookings, allotments, analytics, masters). Booking + Allotment modules alone added ~300 lines.

ObjectId leak pattern: pymongo's insert_one mutates the dict passed in, adding _id. The codebase has multiple endpoints that insert then return the same dict — add a tiny helper `_clean(doc)` that pops _id, or always re-fetch with {'_id': 0} projection (followups endpoint already does this, bookings/payments/allotment did not).
create_booking checks `if not final_price` — this treats final_deal_price == 0 as missing; likely fine but explicit is better.
POST /bookings/{bid}/allotment sets status='Allotted' immediately while ALLOTMENT_STATUSES = [Pending, Allotted] — why have 'Pending' if we never create in that state?
add_payment cap check uses `+0.01` tolerance — acceptable, but document magic number.
PUT /bookings/{bid} silently strips booking_date/status for non-admins — consider returning 403 instead so the UI/user knows the change was rejected.
cancel_booking allows admin to cancel even after status already == 'Cancelled' (no idempotency guard) — minor.
confirm_booking uses `paid < float(booking['booking_amount'])` — floating point comparison; consider Decimal or explicit tolerance.
No index on bookings.status which is queried with {'status': {'$ne':'Cancelled'}} on every create — acceptable for low volume, revisit at scale.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (3 one-line fixes: doc.pop('_id', None) after insert_one in create_booking, add_payment, create_allotment)

success_rate.backend: 100% (18/18 Module 5 & 6 tests; 14/14 Module 3 & 4 regression after fix)

test_credentials: superadmin@dealer.com/super123, admin@dealer.com/admin123 (Bilimora), sales1@dealer.com/sales123 (Bilimora, assigned owner), sales3@dealer.com/sales123 (Chikhli, cross-branch RBAC) — all verified.
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_M56_-prefixed leads, bookings, payments, allotments via public API only. No direct DB writes in this suite. Each suite run creates ~14 leads + bookings + allotments; cleanup is not done (can be added in a fixture teardown later).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression suite to run before any future iteration: test_modules_3_4.py (14) + test_modules_5_6.py (18). The Module 3/4 at_risk test sleeps 62s (FOLLOWUP_MIN_GAP_SECONDS=60). Use `_make_booking_ready_lead()` helper in test_modules_5_6.py to quickly get a lead with deal.final_deal_price=71000 and discount=2000 (no approval needed). Creating a booking via POST /leads/{lid}/booking auto-advances lead.stage to Booking bypassing /stage's Connected-followup requirement. Chassis numbers are stored uppercase — uniqueness is enforced via unique index and application-level check. PUT /allotments requires admin/super_admin (require_roles decorator).
rca of the issue: Motor/PyMongo's insert_one() mutates the input dict in place to add _id (BSON ObjectId). create_booking, add_payment, and create_allotment all returned that same dict to FastAPI, whose jsonable_encoder then tried to iterate ObjectId and raised 500. Reproduction: POST /api/leads/{lid}/booking with valid body -> 500 Internal Server Error, backend log: `TypeError: 'ObjectId' object is not iterable`. Mitigation applied: `doc.pop('_id', None)` immediately after each insert_one. A broader fix would add a single helper or always use `await db.col.find_one({'id': id}, {'_id': 0})` after insert.
